content:
The user wants me to extract the table from the provided image. **1. Analyze the image:** - The image is a technical specification sheet for a "Chip Resistor Surface Mount". - It contains a large table with columns for "TEST", "TEST METHOD", "PROCEDURE", and "REQUIREMENTS". - The table is split into two main parts visually, but it's one continuous table structure. - There are sub-columns under "REQUIREMENTS" for the "Noise" test. **2. Structure the table:** - **Header:** - YAGEO logo - Product specification - Chip Resistor Surface Mount | RC | SERIES | 0805 (Pb Free) - Page number 8/10 - **Table Columns:** - TEST - TEST METHOD - PROCEDURE - REQUIREMENTS (This column has a nested structure for the "Noise" row) - **Rows:** 1. **Solderability:** - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 208A; IEC 60115-1 4.17 - Procedure: Solder bath at 245±3 °C; Dipping time: 2±0.5 seconds - Requirements: Well tinned (≥95% covered); No visible damage 2. **Bending Strength:** - Test Method: JIS C 5202.6.14; IEC 60115-1 4.15 - Procedure: Resistors mounted on a 90 mm glass epoxy resin PCB (FR4); Bending: 5 mm - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol.; No visible damage 3. **Resistance to Solvent:** - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 215; IEC 60115-1 4.29 - Procedure: Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH) or dichloromethane (CH₂Cl₂) followed by brushing - Requirements: No smeared 4. **Noise:** - Test Method: JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12 - Procedure: Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied. - Requirements: This section is split into "Resistors range" and "Value". - R < 100 Ω: 10 dB - 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ: 20 dB - 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ: 30 dB - 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ: 40 dB - 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ: 46 dB - 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ: 48 dB 5. **Humidity (steady state):** - Test Method: JIS C 5202 7.5; IEC 60115-8 4.24.8 - Procedure: 1,000 hours; 40±2 °C; 93(+2/-3)% RH; RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. 6. **Leaching:** - Test Method: EIA/IS 4.13B; IEC 60115-8 4.18 - Procedure: Solder bath at 260±5 °C; Dipping time: 30±1 seconds - Requirements: No visible damage 7. **Intermittent Overload:** - Test Method: JIS C 5202 5.8 - Procedure: At room temperature; 2.5 × RCWV applied for 1 second on and 25 seconds off; total 10,000 cycles - Requirements: ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. 8. **Resistance to Vibration:** - Test Method: On request - Procedure: On request - Requirements: (Empty) 9. **Moisture Resistance Heat:** - Test Method: MIL-STD-202F-method 106F; IEC 60115-1 4.24.2 - Procedure: 42 cycles; total 1,000 hours; Shown as figure 9 - Requirements: ±(0.5%+0.05Ω) for 1% tol.; ±(2.0%+0.05Ω) for 5% tol.; No visible damage **3. Formatting:** - Use Markdown for the table. - Ensure nested tables or lists are used where appropriate (like for the Noise requirements). **4. Refinement:** - Double check values and units. - Ensure the "Noise" section is clearly represented. It looks like a sub-table. Let's construct the Markdown. ```markdown | TEST | TEST METHOD | PROCEDURE | REQUIREMENTS | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Solderability | MIL-STD-202F-method 208A; IEC 60115-1 4.17 | Solder bath at 245±3 °C Dipping time: 2±0.5 seconds | Well tinned (≥95% covered) No visible damage | | Bending Strength | JIS C 5202.6.14; IEC 60115-1 4.15 | Resistors mounted on a 90 mm glass epoxy resin PCB (FR4) Bending: 5 mm | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | | Resistance to Solvent | MIL-STD-202F-method 215; IEC 60115-1 4.29 | Isopropylalcohol (C₃H₇OH) or dichloromethane (CH₂Cl₂) followed by brushing | No smeared | | Noise | JIS C 5202 5.9; IEC 60115-1 4.12 | Maximum voltage (Vᵣₘₛ) applied. | Resistors range | Value | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | | | | R < 100 Ω | 10 dB | | | | | 100 Ω ≤ R < 1 KΩ | 20 dB | | | | | 1 KΩ ≤ R < 10 KΩ | 30 dB | | | | | 10 KΩ ≤ R < 100 KΩ | 40 dB | | | | | 100 KΩ ≤ R < 1 MΩ | 46 dB | | | | | 1 MΩ ≤ R ≤ 22 MΩ | 48 dB | | Humidity (steady state) | JIS C 5202 7.5; IEC 60115-8 4.24.8 | 1,000 hours; 40±2 °C; 93(+2/-3)% RH RCWV applied for 1.5 hours on and 0.5 hour off | ±(0.5%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Leaching | EIA/IS 4.13B; IEC 60115-8 4.18 | Solder bath at 260±5 °C Dipping time: 30±1 seconds | No visible damage | | Intermittent Overload | JIS C 5202 5.8 | At room temperature; 2.5 × RCWV applied for 1 second on and 25 seconds off; total 10,000 cycles | ±(1.0%+0.05 Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05 Ω) for 5% tol. | | Resistance to Vibration | On request | On request | | | Moisture Resistance Heat | MIL-STD-202F-method 106F; IEC 60115-1 4.24.2 | 42 cycles; total 1,000 hours Shown as figure 9 | ±(0.5%+0.05Ω) for 1% tol. ±(2.0%+0.05Ω) for 5% tol. No visible damage | ``` Wait, the "Noise" section is a bit tricky. It's not a separate row, but rather the "REQUIREMENTS" cell for the "Noise" row contains a nested table. I will represent it as such. Also, check the footer info. - Sep 03, 2004 V.2 - www.yageo.com Final check of the content. - "Resistors range" and "Value" headers are inside the Requirements cell for the Noise row. - The values align correctly.
